title: Green etiquette: lines, marks, and the flagstick
microFact: Never walk on the invisible line between anyone's ball and the hole. It's the most sacred real estate in golf.
lesson:
The green has the densest etiquette in golf:

• THE LINE: every player has an imaginary putting line from ball to hole. Never step on anyone's line — your spikes dent the surface. Walk around, or step OVER it if you must.
• MARKING: on the green you may lift your ball after placing a MARKER (coin or flat disc) directly behind it. Mark whenever your ball might distract another player or sits near their line.
• THE FLAGSTICK: since 2019 you may putt with it in. If a player asks you to TEND it (hold it and pull as the ball approaches) or remove it, closest player to the hole usually does the job. Lay a removed flag gently on the fringe, never drop it on the green.
• SHADOWS: don't let your shadow fall across someone's line or the hole while they putt.
• Stand still and silent, outside their field of vision.

First ball in the hole? Don't celebrate across someone's unfinished putt.

quiz.question: Since the 2019 rules update, putting with the flagstick left in the hole is…
quiz.explanation: It's fully legal now. Many players leave it in for pace; remove or tend it if a partner asks.
